# Questions in Livestream 57

Live Stream #57: This Business Is Our Fifty-Seven

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=515" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:35</a> | Does 2 Kings 19:35 conflict with Hezekiah still appearing as an Assyrian vassal, or is that a revision of the Bible? | No. He treated the event as historically complex rather than contradictory: Sennacherib failed to win cleanly, but the evidence does not let us reduce the whole episode to a simple reversal. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=807" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:27</a> | Is it true that we have actually found mummies of ancient Egyptian pharaohs? | Yes. He said many royal mummies survive, especially from Dynasties 17 through 19, with Ramesses II and Tutankhamun as famous examples. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=1044" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:24</a> | What can be said about the historical context of Jonah, the Assyrian Empire in that period, and its king? | He placed Jonah in the reign of Jeroboam II, when Assyria under Shalmaneser IV and Ashur-dan III was weakened, pressured, and not yet in its later imperial resurgence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=1300" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:40</a> | Does early Hebrew use of modified hieratic numerals point to Israelite origins in Egypt? | Not necessarily by itself. He said it shows Egyptian influence, but that influence could also have come through Egyptian-controlled Canaanite settings and garrison scribal practice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=1487" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:47</a> | Are the hailstones in Joshua 10:11 really plausible as a deadly battlefield event? | Yes. He argued that rare but severe hail could kill large numbers, especially against lightly protected Bronze Age troops. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=1837" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:37</a> | Are there ancient sources showing that `40 years` and `1,000 years` can function idiomatically rather than as strict literals? | Yes. He used the Mesha Stele for `40` as an idiomatic number and said both biblical and patristic usage support `1,000` as a figure for a very long span. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=2057" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:17</a> | Can a global-flood view be steel-manned in a way that still fits the pyramids? | He said American young-earth chronology runs into major historical problems with the pyramids, though broader or less rigid flood models are easier to reconcile. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=2642" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">44:02</a> | Could he explain the ba and ka distinction in ancient Egyptian thought? | He said the ba is the personality, emotions, passions, and sense of humor; the ka is life energy or living essence; and the akh is the intellectual component. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=2847" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:27</a> | To what extent were Egyptian pharaohs worshiped as living gods during their lifetimes? | He said this was really true only from Amenhotep III through the end of Dynasty 20; before and after that, kings were more like gods in potential until death. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=3092" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:32</a> | What is his take on the missing verses in Jeremiah's Dead Sea Scroll material, and how do they figure in terms of inspiration? | He said he had not studied Jeremiah's authorship and textual issues enough to give a useful answer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=3190" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">53:10</a> | Where do the Sea Peoples' invasions fit into the conquest of Canaan? | He agreed they may have softened Canaanite targets, with Sea Peoples pressing from the coast while Israelites pressed from the east and Egypt's control weakened. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=3444" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:24</a> | Does the similarity between the bronze serpent and the staff of Asclepius point to Jewish influence on Greek culture and religion? | Not necessarily. He favored convergent symbolism, while allowing possible Egyptian or Jewish influence but saying the origin is hard to prove. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=3572" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:32</a> | If leprosy lives in Egypt's deserts, why not in deserts such as Arizona and Nevada, and what does it live on? | He said Hansen's disease does exist broadly, including North America, but transmission is limited; soil exposure, poverty, footwear, and other vectors affect outbreaks. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=3769" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:49</a> | During whose judgeship did Merneptah attack Israel, and why would Judges not mention it? | He placed it during the conquest rather than the judges period, said Joshua would be the more likely setting, and noted biblical chroniclers often omit historically important events. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=4175" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:35</a> | How could there be Nephilim before and after the flood? | He rejected Heiser's angelic-hybrid view and connected Nephilim to fallen priestly figures produced by broken celibacy vows, so a similar social-religious pattern could recur after the flood. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=4472" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:32</a> | How does that view of the Nephilim harmonize with them being characterized as men of renown? | He pointed to figures such as Sargon of Akkad as an analogy: offspring of broken priestly vows could be feared as powerful, disruptive, and socially consequential. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=4771" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:19:31</a> | How can a preacher cover more Bible without simply preaching faster? | He said the aim should be deeper expository preaching, not speed: take smaller passages, explain them carefully, and balance Old and New Testament teaching over time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=5216" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:56</a> | What books, scholars, or resources would he recommend for studying ancient Near Eastern history and religion? | He recommended resources by Kitchen, John Walton, Richard Hess, James Hoffmeier, Brent Strawn, and his own Ark of the Covenant book, among others. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=5392" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:52</a> | How should Mark 10:18 be understood in a Trinitarian context when Unitarians use it as evidence? | He said Jesus is not denying his goodness or deity; he is using a rhetorical question to expose the rich young ruler's flawed view of goodness and self-righteousness. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=5931" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:51</a> | Is Leviticus 18:22 only about men with boys rather than condemning male same-sex relations generally? | He said the Hebrew does not refer to boys, is directed to men concerning males, and uses the strongest category of condemnation, though he answered cautiously because of platform rules. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=6323" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:23</a> | Could `40` be idiomatic for a generation, making Moses' 120 years about three generations? | He said that is possible and better than rigid literalism in some cases, but `40` is more broadly an idiom for an uncertain long time and does not always mean a generation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=6607" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:07</a> | Does the biblical text clearly say Pharaoh and all his army drowned in the Reed Sea? | He said no. He argued that Psalm 136 speaks poetically of God shaking off Pharaoh and his army, while Exodus and archaeology do not require Pharaoh himself or Egypt's whole army to have drowned. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=7321" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:02:01</a> | Did Yeshua lie at the wedding at Cana when he told Mary that his time had not yet come? | He said no. Jesus was signaling that the miracle was a one-off, not the main timing or purpose of his ministry, while still honoring his mother and saving the wedding party from shame. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=7787" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:09:47</a> | Is there a contradiction between Genesis 1:28's command to multiply and 1 Corinthians 7's claim that being unmarried can be better? | No. He said Genesis 1 concerns the creation mandate to fill the earth, while 1 Corinthians 7 concerns undistracted devotion to God; Paul still says marriage is not sin. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=8341" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:01</a> | What is fantasy, truth, or possible evidence for angelic infiltration in ancient aliens, ghosts, and related phenomena? | He dismissed ancient aliens as fantasy but treated ghost phenomena as complex, with some apparitions, hauntings, and poltergeist-like activity possibly involving spiritual beings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=8803" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:43</a> | Who were Yeshua's brothers, and how were they related to him? | He said the answer depends heavily on denominational tradition: Catholic cousins, Orthodox Joseph's children from an earlier marriage, or Protestant younger children of Joseph and Mary; he favored the Orthodox or Protestant options as stronger than the cousin view. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=9105" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:31:45</a> | Could a dream of a deceased Christian parent appearing healed be genuine? | He said it is possible that someone dead in Christ could appear for a special purpose, while warning that Scripture forbids seeking contact with the dead. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=9341" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:41</a> | What are his biggest unanswered questions in biblical studies? | He highlighted early Hebrew paleography, identifying Abraham's Dynasty 13 pharaoh, clarifying the later Exodus route, the Ark's side decoration, and the flood's timing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=9667" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:41:07</a> | Does Deuteronomy 23:15-16 mean the Mosaic law allows a slave to leave if they choose? | He said the passage appears to prohibit returning an escaped slave, but cautioned that Israelite slave law is specialized and the practical consequences could differ by the kind of slavery involved. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=10344" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:24</a> | What does he think of John Walton's book on demons? | He had not read it yet, though he was curious about it and noted he was behind on conference book purchases. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=10719" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:58:39</a> | Was Elisha like Thor after recovering the lost axe head from the Jordan River? | He said the point is not the axe head itself but water manipulation as a prophetic sign, comparable to Moses, Joshua, Elijah, Elisha, and Egyptian traditions about wise men. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=11226" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:07:06</a> | Is John Walton right that demons in the Bible are not ontologically real but only literary devices? | He rejected that view, saying demons are real and similar to angels in ontology, with differences of alignment and corrupted function. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=11415" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:10:15</a> | Is Jesus the ultimate water bender because he changed water into wine? | He treated the comment as having a real point: changing water into wine is a form of water manipulation that demonstrates Jesus' prophetic status as equal to Moses. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=11499" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:11:39</a> | Is it crazy that a deceased mother explained the Trinity in a dream after prayer and fasting? | He said not necessarily, but dreams require discernment because some may be ordinary subconscious activity, some genuine communication, and some deceptive spiritual manipulation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=12303" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:25:03</a> | From his study, is praying to the saints biblical? | He said Scripture does not clearly settle it and the question depends on church tradition; he was more comfortable praying directly to God and warned that not every alleged saint may truly be safe to invoke. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/b3ytxeDVHGg?t=12946" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:35:46</a> | Did the ancient Egyptians ever find dinosaur fossils or other prehistoric beasts? | Generally no. He said they clearly encountered fossil shells and brachiopods at places like Giza, but not the kinds of large dinosaur remains people usually imagine. |
